sched.title: Schedule
sched.body: Base cron four times per market day (ET, weekdays) with small jitter. Optional extra ET slots can be enabled. A position-health interval can run trail giveback and related automation without an LLM. An end-of-day summary runs after the regular session. The main cycle is gated to regular trading hours.

reg.title: Regime (pre-brain)
reg.body: One small-model JSON verdict per TTL window using cross-asset inputs: benchmark equity slices (e.g. broad index ETFs), volatility, macro, prediction markets, positioning, sentiment, and similar feeds. Bearish conviction above a threshold can merge inverse or volatility tickers into the dynamic watchlist when not overridden by a static list. The verdict attaches to every per-symbol snapshot for that cycle.

wl.title: Watchlist
wl.body: Builds the symbol universe from a capped screener, fixed anchors, optional bearish hedge names from the regime verdict, and optionally every open underlying. Cached by regime fingerprint with optional quality filters.

feed.title: Snapshot builder
feed.body: Historical daily bars plus custom indicators and live fields; portfolio context and sector hints; data-quality flags; optional cycle-level regime object when the main loop passes it in. The regime step also consults benchmark slices via the same snapshot machinery for cross-asset context.

enr.title: Enrichment layer
enr.body: Mutates the snapshot with earnings, news, macro, volatility, prediction markets, sentiment, positioning, filings, optional news APIs, social signals, and options context.

snap.title: Market snapshot
snap.body: Single dict passed to the model: price, indicators, account, position, portfolio context, data quality, optional sector label, optional regime verdict, and enrichers.

trd.title: Trader (Haiku)
trd.body: Directional persona proposes one structured JSON trade per symbol. Parse failures auto-retry then fall back to a safe hold shell so one bad response cannot halt the cycle.

risk.title: Risk engine
risk.body: Tier-1 structural safety (loss limits, malformed fields, allowlists). Tier-2 policy: confidence floor, cooldowns, pattern-day-trade ceiling, sector caps, earnings and technical filters, option minimum days to expiry, overnight holds, intraday giveback halts. A soft policy mode can downgrade some tier-2 blocks to warnings for experiments.

exe.title: Executor
exe.body: Bracket GTC stock buys; simple market sells to close; single-leg options with liquidity checks on bid/ask when enabled.

journal.title: Journal
journal.body: SQLite row plus JSONL line with snapshot excerpt, raw and final decision, execute result, and debate metadata. Logs can rotate by max size with retention of rotated siblings.

aMD.title: Alpaca market data
aMD.body: Historical daily bars and live snapshot for price, volume, VWAP, and previous close.

aTR.title: Alpaca trading API
aTR.body: Account equity, positions, day-trade count, and screener client for the dynamic watchlist.

aOPT.title: Alpaca options
aOPT.body: Nearest-expiry at-the-money ring with implied volatility and mid for options-aware decisions.

rss.title: Headlines
rss.body: RSS headlines per symbol or configured feeds (cached).

fred.title: FRED macro
fred.body: Treasury curve, inflation, and unemployment series when an API key is configured.

vix.title: VIX
vix.body: Spot level and a simple volatility regime label from recent closes.

kal.title: Kalshi
kal.body: Macro series markets via public API.

poly.title: Polymarket
poly.body: High-volume macro-tilted markets from a public API.

stw.title: StockTwits
stw.body: Retail stream bullish/bearish mix for the active symbol.

cotF.title: CFTC COT
cotF.body: Weekly non-commercial net positioning for a small futures panel.

fh.title: Finnhub
fh.body: Optional earnings calendar, recommendations, and news sentiment when configured with an API key.

mktx.title: Marketaux
mktx.body: Optional headlines with entity sentiment when configured with an API key.

rdd.title: Reddit
rdd.body: Optional OAuth search hits mentioning the symbol when OAuth credentials are configured.

qv.title: QuiverQuant
qv.body: Congressional trading and lobbying endpoints with token auth per vendor docs.

alp.title: Broker
alp.body: Paper or live account receiving orders.

db.title: SQLite
db.body: Trades, risk state, and trail state tables. Connections use WAL mode and a busy timeout so a read-only viewer can stay open while the bot writes.

jsl.title: JSONL log
jsl.body: Append-only line log mirroring row metadata with optional rotation and pruning.

web.title: Operator viewer
web.body: Read-only web app for cycles, flow diagram, and positions — separate from this public educational site.

hc.title: Healthchecks.io
hc.body: End-of-cycle success/failure ping for dead-man monitoring.

met.title: Metrics JSONL
met.body: Optional path appends one JSON object per finished cycle with timing and compact regime summary.

eodMD.title: EOD digest
eodMD.body: After the cash session, aggregates journal rows and account equity into stable and dated markdown reports.

crSun.title: Weekly cron
crSun.body: Example Sunday job entrypoint for the weekly review.

wrpy.title: Weekly review
wrpy.body: Aggregates JSONL and SQLite, attaches an operating snapshot for prompt context, calls a larger model once, and writes review markdown, feedback bullets, and blog artifacts.

mdLatest.title: Weekly report file
mdLatest.body: Stable latest-review markdown plus dated copies and JSON sidecar.

blogUI.title: Blog page
blogUI.body: Human-readable weekly post with charts in the operator viewer.

fbmd.title: Trader feedback
fbmd.body: Short bullets after weekly review; the brain prepends when fresh for historical critique (not live market data).

SRC.title: External feeds
SRC.body: Logical grouping of third-party and broker-provided inputs.

PIPE.title: Pipeline
PIPE.body: Pre-cycle regime and watchlist, per-symbol snapshot build, model decision, risk, execution, and journaling.

SINK.title: Sinks
SINK.body: Persistence, metrics, external monitoring, and operator-facing surfaces.

WKY.title: Weekly subgraph
WKY.body: Offline aggregation and long-form reporting path.

BRN.title: Brain subgraph
BRN.body: Structured JSON decision inside the per-symbol pipeline.
